steps.title: Fastest path: mark the relationships first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships in the setup first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify supplementary angles from parallel lines
steps.content:
When parallel lines are cut by a transversal, an acute angle and the adjacent obtuse angle are supplementary. They must sum to 180°:
$$4x + 49 + 6x + 81 = 180$$

steps.title: Solve for x and find the angle measures
steps.content:
Combine like terms:
$$10x + 130 = 180$$
$$10x = 50$$
$$x = 5$$
Now find the angle measures:
Acute angle = $4(5) + 49 = 69^{\circ}$
Obtuse angle = $6(5) + 81 = 111^{\circ}$

steps.title: Calculate the sum of 2 acute and 3 obtuse angles
steps.content:
All acute angles in this configuration equal 69°, and all obtuse angles equal 111°. The sum requested is:
$$2(69^{\circ}) + 3(111^{\circ}) = 138^{\circ} + 333^{\circ} = 471^{\circ}$$
